implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0

completion_promises.criterion_text: A search returns a single assembled context block (not a raw list).
completion_promises.evidence: assemble_context() returns AssemblyResult with single context_block string. Tests test_empty_results_returns_empty_block and test_single_item_within_budget verify this.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The assembled block never exceeds the token budget, measured with tiktoken cl100k_base. (AC-1)
completion_promises.evidence: Token measurement uses tiktoken.get_encoding('cl100k_base'). Assembly loop checks candidate_tokens <= token_budget before accepting items. Test test_assembled_block_never_exceeds_budget passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A block whose assembled size is exactly the budget (e.g. 2000) is returned in full and measures exactly the budget. (boundary: just-inside)
completion_promises.evidence: Assembly uses <= comparison, accepting content exactly at budget. Test test_exactly_at_budget_returns_full verifies boundary compliance.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Content that would push the block past the budget is dropped rather than overflowing; the lowest-ranked memories are the ones omitted. (boundary: 2100 → drop lowest)
completion_promises.evidence: When candidate_tokens > token_budget, loop breaks, leaving lower-ranked items unprocessed. Test test_over_budget_drops_lowest_ranked passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A zero token budget returns an empty context block (not an error); coverage reports nothing filled.
completion_promises.evidence: Early return when token_budget == 0, returning AssemblyResult with empty context_block, coverage_score=0.0. Test test_zero_budget_returns_empty_block passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A single memory larger than the entire budget is omitted whole, not truncated mid-content; an empty block is returned and coverage reports the budget could not be filled. (ASSUM-009, low confidence — carried as open)
completion_promises.evidence: No content truncation logic. If first item exceeds budget, rejected in first iteration, loop terminates with empty block. Test test_single_memory_larger_than_budget_omitted passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: When the budget forces a cut, the higher-ranked memory is kept and the lower-ranked one omitted (a highly relevant warning beats a barely relevant overview).
completion_promises.evidence: Processes ranked_results in order (relevance descending), accepting items until budget exhausted. Test test_higher_ranked_kept_over_lower passes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The result reports a coverage score: the fraction (0.0–1.0) of the budget filled, plus the set of payload types that contributed to the block.
completion_promises.evidence: AssemblyResult includes coverage_score (tokens_used / token_budget) and contributing_types set from natural_key prefixes. Tests test_coverage_score_reported and test_contributing_types_extracted pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A search that cannot fill the budget reports partial coverage honestly (not padded).
completion_promises.evidence: Coverage calculated as actual tokens_used / token_budget, no padding. Test test_partial_coverage_reported_honestly verifies coverage < 1.0 when unfilled.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Repeated searches over an unchanged corpus return an identical assembled block (assembly is deterministic given identical ranked input).
completion_promises.evidence: Deterministic assembly: processes in order, fixed encoding, no randomness or time-dependence. Test test_deterministic_assembly verifies identical repeated results.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Executed 'ruff check src/fleet_memory/retrieval/' - result: 'All checks passed!' Implementation follows type hints, docstrings, PEP 8 conventions.
